page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.xlarge is an ARM64-based compute-optimized instance powered by a 2.8 GHz Ampere Altra processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, paired with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance operates on a KVM hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core and single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng scores of 7275.05 and 1824.29 respectively. Large-block uncached memory read and write speeds are moderate, measuring 55.26 GB/sec and 31.38 GB/sec. However, cryptographic and compression tasks show weaker performance, as evidenced by a Passmark encryption score of 649.93 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 23.68 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture of the Ampere Altra processor provides a power-efficient, dedicated-core hardware profile suitable for scale-out workloads. The instance is well-suited for workloads such as text processing, lightweight database operations, static web serving, and ARM64 application development or testing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores powered by a 2.8 GHz Ampere Altra processor. Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show average multi-core CPU performance and moderate large-block memory bandwidth, though cryptographic and compression workloads exhibit lower throughput. The dedicated-core ARM architecture offers a cost-efficient profile for scale-out environments. It is best suited for text processing, lightweight database operations, static web serving, and ARM64 development and testing.

tagline: An ARM64-powered compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Ampere Altra cores for efficient scale-out workloads and lightweight database operations.

Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a compute-optimized 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Delivers 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPU accelerators
Demonstrates average multi-core CPU performance and moderate uncached memory bandwidth
